brief: Opportunities are heavily concentrated in Brooklyn R7A and R7-2 zoning corridors where 485-x tax exemptions combined with Section 8 VPS rents yield strong cash-on-cash returns between 12.4% and 13.7%. Out of 1,271 scanned parcels, 920 pencil, but top-tier yields are driven by city-owned lots with significant land cost headroom. Bronx program plays offer developer fees up to $280,948 via ELLA/4% LIHTC stacks, though estimated land costs currently exceed walk-away thresholds without competitive subsidies.

targets.play: Develop a 12-unit residential project in Brooklyn leveraging 485-x tax exemptions and Section 8 VPS rents to achieve a 13.7% cash-on-cash yield.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $591,738 against a $1,588,048 walk-away threshold provides $996,310 in land price headroom.
targets.risk: Property is city-owned, requiring acquisition through the competitive HPD disposition RFP process rather than a private seller transaction.

targets.play: Execute a 10-unit build in an R7-2 zone utilizing 485-x tax benefits and Section 8 VPS rents targeting a 12.7% cash-on-cash yield.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost sits at $569,141 versus a $1,525,802 walk-away ceiling, leaving $956,661 of headroom.
targets.risk: Requires zoning verification for R7-2 envelope efficiency and navigating HPD disposition procedures.

targets.play: Construct a 14-unit R7A project under the 485-x and Section 8 VPS framework delivering a 12.4% cash-on-cash return.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $690,612 against a walk-away valuation of $1,760,767 yields $1,070,155 in headroom.
targets.risk: Site condition diligence and navigating public HPD/EDC disposition timing.

targets.play: Build a 14-unit R6A project in Queens utilizing 485-x and Section 8 VPS rents to achieve an 11.2% cash-on-cash yield.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost is $622,014 compared to a $1,531,605 walk-away limit, creating $909,591 in headroom.
targets.risk: Subject to city-owned site disposition through HPD/EDC and local Queens zoning verification.

targets.play: Pursue an 8-unit all-affordable Bronx development using an ELLA / 4% LIHTC stack to generate a $280,948 developer fee.
targets.negotiation: Estimated land cost of $531,360 exceeds the $196,360 walk-away land value by $335,000, meaning land must be deeply discounted or subsidized by the city.
targets.risk: Requires securing a highly competitive subsidy award and favorable city disposition terms to close the financial gap.

process_note: Next steps require conducting preliminary zoning verification and physical site inspections while tracking HPD/EDC RFP pipeline releases for the city-owned parcels.
disclaimer: AI interpretation of the engine's real screening output. Estimates pending diligence — not investment advice.
